name: OSHA 29 CFR 1910 Subpart E – Means of Egress / Emergency Plans
source_authority: U.S. Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)

rules.label: Means of Egress – Design
rules.notes_template: Verify exit routes are permanent, separated by fire-resistance-rated construction, with self-closing fire doors; door swing direction, capacity, width, headroom comply.
rules.citation: 29 CFR 1910.36 – Design and construction requirements for exit routes (permanent, fire-resistance-rated, two routes where reasonably feasible, sufficient capacity, etc.).
rules.remediation: Document exit-route design (permanent construction, separation, capacity, headroom, signage).

rules.label: Means of Egress – Maintenance
rules.notes_template: Verify exit routes free of explosive/flammable furnishings, kept unobstructed, adequately lit, marked with 'EXIT' signs visible from any direction.
rules.citation: 29 CFR 1910.37 – Exit routes shall be unobstructed, properly lit, and marked with EXIT signs.
rules.remediation: Add maintenance program and signage to construction documents and operations plan.

rules.label: Emergency Action Plan
rules.notes_template: Verify a written EAP for employers > 10 employees: alarm, evacuation, accounting for personnel, rescue/medical duties, contact info; trained on hire and on changes.
rules.citation: 29 CFR 1910.38 – Emergency action plan required (written for > 10 employees) with specified minimum content and training.
rules.remediation: Develop and document a §1910.38-compliant EAP; train employees.

rules.label: Fire Prevention Plan
rules.notes_template: Verify written fire-prevention plan (where required) covering fuel sources, ignition controls, housekeeping, fire-prevention equipment maintenance, and employee training.
rules.citation: 29 CFR 1910.39 – Fire prevention plan required by certain other OSHA standards; written for employers > 10 employees.
rules.remediation: Develop a written fire-prevention plan covering hazards on site.
